I see that Pearton is not with you. Met an unfortunate end, I take it?
Thankfully the only family he had died a few weeks ago, so we won't have to worry about paying any death benefits. Did you find the shells, at least?
<sprite name=b> He asked me to give them to you along with his resignation.
He what!? Well, I can't say I'm surprised. Cordin was the only one who could keep him grounded... Thank you for your assistance in this matter, payment is in order.
<sprite name=?s> Appreciate it.
Better live than dead, I suppose.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Grrrrrr...
Bark!
Woof!
Hello! Apologies if we don't have the items youΓÇÖre looking for ΓÇô between the rumors of Osprix, Liath's orders, and Lagon's storms; things have been a little chaotic lately!
<sprite name=coin> Shop.
<sprite name=d> Nevermind, then.
*Touching the pearl before you sends a ripple across the pool to the other altars to your right and left... then nothing happens*
<sprite name=d> I should go.
*Touching the pearl sends a ripple across the pool to the other altars to your right and left, which create ripples of their own, repeating in a chain reaction as the pool begins to glow brighter*
<sprite name=?> *Step into the pool*
*The altar in front of you holds a broken moon-shaped symbol; the damage seems recent*
*You wonder if the missing half is somewhere on this islandΓÇª*
<sprite name=d> *Leave*
*The altar in front of you holds a broken moon-shaped symbol; the damage seems recent*
*You wonder if the missing half is somewhere on this islandΓÇª*
<sprite name=d> *Leave*
*The altar in front of you holds a broken moon-shaped symbol; the moon fragment you found appears to fit it perfectly*
<sprite name=?> *Place moon fragment*
*The altar in front of you holds a broken moon-shaped symbol; the moon fragment you found appears to fit it perfectly*
<sprite name=?> *Place moon fragment*
What did I tell you about coming here?
I shattered the moon fragments that power the pool for a reason. Now go ΓÇô IΓÇÖm leaving to consult with Lagon about Rahyeh. Do not let me find you here again.
<sprite name=?> Ugh, fine.
I shattered the moon fragments that power the pool for a reason. Now go ΓÇô IΓÇÖm leaving to consult with Lagon about Rahyeh. Do not let me find you back.
<sprite name=?> I get it.
<sprite name=?> Goodbye.
You've come at the worst time. We're packing up before more invaders break through. But if you really want somethingΓÇª make it quick.
<sprite name=coin> Shop.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Trade up here while the Heoboreans and Solarum are bickering has beenΓÇª difficult.
<sprite name=d> Interesting.
<sprite name=b> The Architect?
Architect Liath. She oversees law and defense in Lagon's domain. Which normally, she does well. But right now? Once Trade Prince Melvern hears of this, heΓÇÖll be livid.
And now the west routes are under siege as well? ItΓÇÖs no wonder the Architect ordered us all to return.
I refuse to retreat to the tundra! Deep Harbor is my home, and I refuse to hide in the wastes like Wengari! If only my damned leg would moveΓÇª
<sprite name=d> Good luck.
Why is this happening? We arenΓÇÖt warriors. WeΓÇÖre ferrymen, fishers and childrenΓÇª Why would Rahyeh want to destroy us?
<sprite name=b> Try to get out quickly.
<sprite name=b> You okay?
LeavingΓÇÖs hardΓÇª TheyΓÇÖve crippled my son. JustΓÇª why? Why us?
<sprite name=d> Good luck.
Saboteurs. They had been sneaking supplies and their damn bombs on the east pier for gods know how long. We never had a chance.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Welcome. Supplies are scarce right now, but we may have something that can help.
<sprite name=coin> Shop.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Any luck convincing them to let us use the damn river? I don't care how many Osprix are swarming, I wonΓÇÖt go back north.
<sprite name=b> Not yet.
<sprite name=b> Just hang tight.
I'm close to grabbing one of these boats and taking it to Thetima myself!
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
If one has no other business here, the Architect recommends sheltering in Thetima. If one wishes to provide additional assistance, the Architect's records are still within her tower.
Please be aware of the risk of damage or destruction upon doing so. If recovered, please deliver them to one of the Architect's assistants in Thetima.
<sprite name=!s> On it.
Entry to the Architect's tower is currently forbidden due to an attempt at invasion. The Architect wishes for me to apologize for the inconvenience.
<sprite name=d> Farewell.
Quickly! Once you're within Thetima's walls, youΓÇÖll be safe. The Architect's defenses will make sure of that.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
<sprite name=?s> I've recovered the books from Liath's Tower.
Thank you! I'm sure she'll be thankful. We'll be able to reward you more once she returns from Lagon's temple.
Even the gods cannot observe what fate lies in store for us. Do not let it bind you.
<sprite name=?> Farewell.
Welcome. I can undo a past action of yours, for a price.
<sprite name=respec> Respecialize Mastery Point allocations.
<sprite name=respec> Respecialize Mastery
Minimus!
Run you stupid mutt!
Leave me!
Help! They just burst through the walls! They're heading to the city, Last Refuge is being overrun!
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
What are these creatures!? They're slaughtering my beasts and driving them mad!
<sprite name=?s> I cleared a path, get out of here!
The Void fell upon our world hundreds of years ago. All-consuming darkness spread across the surface and drove us into these caves, the last city of humanity. Last Refuge.
<sprite name=b> What's going on? What is the Void?
<sprite name=?> How are you planning to deal with the Void?
Elder Pannion is an expert at using the shard. He can weaponize it against the Void.
<sprite name=?> What can I do?
Everyone here is either too old or too injured to go warn Elder Pannion... except for you.
<sprite name=!> Accept.
You... I can sense that you have a shard of the Epoch, but that is impossible. Who are you?
<sprite name=?> *Tell Elder Gaspar how you gained the shard.*
I see... We have relied on the power of our shard for ages, always assuming the others were lost, and it seems, only tapping into a fraction of its potential.
<sprite name=b> What are the shards?
They are the shards of the Epoch, a relic said to have power over fate itself, and after hearing your story, I have no doubt in this now.
You were brought forward in time by the shard... if you want to return to your time, we will need to use them together. If the Void does not consume us first of course.
It was broken into three shards long ago. We relied on one of them to sustain this place ever since the Void destroyed the surface.
We thought the other shards were lost... but with yours, we may have hope of surviving this.
Now that the Void has finally found us, it will reach this place and snuff us out.
This is a dark day. ThereΓÇÖs no telling how many have been lost.

The last I heard the Armoury was being overwhelmed as well, if we are to hole up here we need to retrieve arms and armor from there before itΓÇÖs too late. In my state IΓÇÖm useless... but you, however, seem capable of fighting.
<sprite name=b> There must be something I can do.
<sprite name=b> If they couldn't best those things maybe they deserved to die.
You will watch your tongue. If you are so strong, then make yourself useful.
<sprite name=!s> Accept.
<sprite name=!s> Accept.
Find my subordinates at the wall and tell them we need to fall back, and to recover as many supplies as we can.
Down in the Armoury, my men are trying to retrieve arms and supplies. Unless you want to see that darkness kill the rest of us, you will aid them.
Oh, you! I know why youΓÇÖre here, and I will accept your thanks by allowing you to repay me with a favor...

I know this looks grim, but we have to keep hope. We must believe that weΓÇÖll survive this, that IΓÇÖll survive this! I believe in our survival so much that I need you to go to my quarters and find my ledger!
<sprite name=b> Allow me to... what?
<sprite name=b> Why do you need a ledger at a time like this?
Oh, just in case any fools think that this disaster somehow erases the debts they owe me. In return, I'll share one of the trinkets I liberated from the slums before I rescued you.
<sprite name=!s> Accept.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
<sprite name=b> Portal?
Yes, yes, a portal, a simple cantrip anyone with half an inkling of arcane aptitude should be able to do in their sleep. If you were clever, you'd be able to conjure one after watching me back there!
Also, I don't want to have to save your hide with portal again, let me show you how to open one yourself.
It will take you back to a place you regard as safe, such as the Council Chambers. Bah!
Wish I had grabbed more books as I ran...
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
No wonder the Elders kept this place to themselves, hell of a view. Hmm, care to try your luck? Found some good stuff on my way up here.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye
<sprite name=coin> Gamble.

Oh my! You're not supposed to be here you cheeky tart you. Hmm yes, didn't expect to find me here did you? To be fair I didn't expect to be found! Why, what would drive you to walk those legs on over to this tucked away little corner here hmm? Exploratory wall hugging, or as some say, hall wugging? Either way, you got here you curious idol of inquisitivness you! Are you ready for your reward?
>Yes! I'm ever so ready!
>No.

Ah, the modest type then! Fare the well I suppose, it's a shame I wasn't able to give you your reward! Oh how I wanted to see your face light up in glee and wonder, glowing with the sort of joy one may only see upon the face of a child unwrapping a long dreamt of gift! But not to worry, I am not offended, go on! Continue thy quest you noble visage you!
>Goodbye

Oh, but are you? This is not an ordinary gift, do not fool yourself, you will not find this wrapped under an arboreal husk on a winter morn, no no, a tiny elf did not craft this boon with its weary splinter marred hands I can assure you. Are you sure you are prepared for this?
> Yes! I can hardly contain my excitement!
> No.

Hahaha, that is the spirit! Eyes open and body ready, a willing receptacle for all that life throws at you! Uncoverer of secrets and accepter of gifts! You halled the wuggs and hugged the walls, the bearer of an explorer's heart! Can you even imagine what awaits you next? Show me! Laugh with me Traveler!!
>HAHAHAHAHAHAHA!
> No.
Your gift..... is..... turning around and playing the Alpha! Have fun! Love you bye!
>Goodbye.
Those... things have already swarmed into the Archive. It was terrible, words can't describe it. The worst though was what happened to those who didn't run, who didn't die.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
What are you doing? Are you heading the same way Pannion did? I beg you do not!
<sprite name=b> Why not?
I saw them... change. I saw that darkness flow into them and make them something different, something horrible. Don't throw your life away like this!
Damn! I can't die now, my work is nearly complete!
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
<sprite name=b> Your work?
Yes, my research into the Immortal Empire! They were a scourge upon this world, but I know I could have gleaned a more balanced power from their knowledge.
If nothing else they proved that death need not be the end and that the aspirations of man could not be constrained by the gods... oh to have lived in their timeΓÇª

This is the Last Archive. Every piece of history, art, even records and journals, everything that could be brought down here to Last Refuge lies here. I do not know how many died trying to ferry this knowledge here. We can not lose this place.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
We can't let the Void enter here, if we lose this place, then we'll have truly lost everything of the old world.
<sprite name=b> What is this place?
Oh by Rahyeh's sooty feathers you're alive! And you have my ledger, you madman! I knew I could count on you, never had any doubt.
<sprite name=b> Didn't you promise me a reward?
What? A reward? Oh, yes yes, here, this old thing won't be worth much soon anyway.
<sprite name=?s> Goodbye.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Hurry now, what is taking you so long? This place could be overrun any minute!
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Yes? What is it now? Trust me, if I find a way out of this mess I'll be sure to call you over and tell you all about it.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
There are good men down there. Please, do not let them die like this.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
By the gods, you made it back safely! With these supplies, we may be able to last just that much longer. Thank you, we are in your debt.
<sprite name=?s> You're welcome.
With these supplies, we may be able to hold out just that much longer. We are counting on you traveler.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Go! Find me in the past! Find Heorot!
<sprite name=d> Right.
Captain Ansel sent you? Thank Eterra! It's too late for most of us but Manus, the Quartermaster, is still somewhere in the Armoury. Please! Find him and he'll help get supplies back to the Captain!
<sprite name=?s> Goodbye.

I knew someone would come to help, I knew it! It's too late for most of us but Manus, the Quartermaster, is till somewhere in the Armoury. Please! Find him and he'll help get supplies back to Captain Ansel!
<sprite name=!s> Accept.
<sprite name=d> Refuse.
Don't worry about me, just find the Quartermaster!
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
You managed to fight them off! Ansel sent you? Thank Eterra, I don't know how much longer I could have lasted. Quick! Let's get back to the Council Chambers!
<sprite name=?s> Goodbye.
You managed to fight them off! Thank Eterra, I don't know how much longer I could have lasted. Quick! Let's get back to the Council Chambers!
<sprite name=!s> Goodbye.
Quick! Let's get back to the Council Chambers!
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
We have little time... please, find Pannion.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
By Eterra you've done it! You've returned the shard! What about Pannion?
<sprite name=?> What can we do now?
Even with both of our shards, this city is lost. Our only hope lies in the Temple of Eterra where the last shard remains.
<sprite name=?> I will find the last shard.
The gods have long left this world, but their memory will give you comfort. Be safe, traveler.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
By reforming the Epoch you will be able to return to your time and take us with you, away from this ruin.
You are the only one who can help us.
Thank you. There is an old passage to the surface, I will dispel the rune that keeps it sealed. The Temple of Eterra is far to the east.
Please, take these idols with you. An idol of Lagon to guard you against chaos and one of Eterra to guide you home, wherever that may be.
Our time is running short, reforming the Epoch is the only hope we have of saving this world.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.

My master, Elder Pannion, came to recruit us to help him bring the fight to the Void, but he came back from our study alone. He tasked me with guarding the entrance to make sure no one left while he went on by himself. Could you please go in and tell me what has happened to the other students?
<sprite name=!> Accept.
<sprite name=b> Why were you not with the others?
I overslept! The one time I overslept, and it's the end of the world! Please, go in there and find out what happened to the other students.
<sprite name=d> Refuse.
You there! May I plead your aid?
<sprite name=b> Yes?
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
I know I am a coward... just please, let me know what is going on in there.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.

You're back! What happened to the other students? Oh by Eterra, this is awful! Thank you, if I had gone into there myself I may have ended up just like them. Here, take this, it is the least I can do.
<sprite name=?> Goodbye.
I don't know if I should wait for Elder Pannion to return or if I should flee to the Council Chambers... What do you think I should do?
<sprite name=b> Flee.
You're right. But, with how close the Void is, would it really matter? I... I feel like my legs can't move...
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
<sprite name=b> Wait.
You're right. I should stay here and tell him what happened to the other students. Thank you.
You. YouΓÇÖre not one of them? IΓÇÖd heard stories that we werenΓÇÖt the only ones, but seeing you here feels soΓÇª
<sprite name=b> What do you mean, ΓÇ£only ones?ΓÇ¥
M-my family and I came here when the Void consumed our land up north. We hoped to find Welryn, only to find... Whatever this place is.
<sprite name=b> Leave?
Yes! I saw how you cut through cultists. If you can do thatΓÇª Can you help us? Make a path for our escape?
<sprite name=!> I can try.
<sprite name=d> You're on your own.
Oh, thank you. The rest are in the catacombs, hiding. Bring them back here so we can make our escape. Thank you ΓÇô by the gods, thank you.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
<sprite name=b> Speak plainly.
We've been hiding in the catacombs underneath ever since. For survival, we have to play the role of cultists, but in realityΓÇª Does this mean we can leave?
Having trouble? The others are in the catacombs, hidingΓÇª like rats. Please, help their legs scurry out.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
My family, you escorted them to freedom! The freedom of oblivion!
<sprite name=?> What!?
IΓÇÖll help you too!
!PH!: Well at this point I should have transformed into a miniboss and tried to squash you BUT if you are reading this then the Quest Designer messed something up. Don't mind me, carry on!
Ta~
Did I hear Elder Gaspar correctly? You're going to the surface? If so, I have a request for you.
<sprite name=b> What is it?
Among the old records dating back to when humanity was first fleeing the void, there are reports of attempts by another group to hide within another cave system not too far from Welryn.
<sprite name=!s> Accept.
There have been attempts to scry, divine, and to even dig to where this sister city, this Lesser Refuge if you would, could be but to no avail. Could you perhaps search for any signs of them?
Oh, have you found any signs of the Lesser Refuge? ...I see. If you do manage to find signs of its existence, please return to me, I must add this to the records while I still have a chance.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Oh, you found the Lesser Refuge? Please! Tell me everything you saw, were there survivors? I see. That is a tragedy, but at least with this tome, we will be able to preserve their memory.
<sprite name=b> They didn't want to be called the Lesser Refuge
Oh? What did they want to be called?
<sprite name=b> The Refuge.
<sprite name=b> Rat City.
Ah, I see... I will honor their last wish in that case.
... Rat... City? Very well. If humanity survives this, we will never forget the memory of Rat City.
<sprite name=?s> Goodbye.
<sprite name=?s> Long live Rat City.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
Thank you again for bringing back the tome. The people of the Refuge are honored, no matter where their souls are now.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
I've been scouring the tome since you returned and I'm not seeing any mentions of Rat City. Are you sure you read that correctly traveler?
<sprite name=b> Yes, keep looking.
Hmm... very well.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
*A large stone table holds an old tome*
<sprite name=b> *Turn to Page 1*
<sprite name=d> *Ignore*
This record has been established to chronicle the history of Magister Wollis and his exodus to the underground, in the face of a creeping, dark substance.
<sprite name=b> *Turn to Page 23*
<sprite name=?s> *Flip to the end*

The Elders of Welryn are fools. TheyΓÇÖve spent so long hiding the nature of the shard from the Immortal Empire. Yet the second those tyrants are at their weakest; they want to flee into the mountains?
I have received word that his study is under attack. We may be out of time.
<sprite name=b> *Turn to Page 76*
WeΓÇÖve found a cave system, not far from the city. Inside, darkness is thereΓÇª along with some weakened Immortal scum.
<sprite name=b> *Turn to Page 202*
*This text appears to be in different handwriting*
<sprite name=?s> *Turn to last page*
For decades, we hid here. The Magister insisted we were in no real danger, only for the Void to sweep in and end us all.
<sprite name=!s> *Take the tome*
<sprite name=b> *Open tome*
*Within the chest you find a myriad of belongings. Among them sits an old tome, most of its pages too damaged to read*
Forget this so-called, ΓÇ£Outcast Queen.ΓÇ¥ What we need is a Lesser Refuge.

I wager that this darkness, this Void as we now call it, was born from one of their foul experiments. The fact they die from it now is justice. Now we wait for the void to dissipateΓÇª and head back to the surface.
How ironic that my last testament would be written in the journal of Wollis. The second those mad Void worshippers found us, he offered us to them.
Funny, because they tore him to pieces all the same. His bones are laying in the back in our city, under how much of that darknessΓÇª only Eterra knows.
We carved homes from this stone. WeΓÇÖve raised children here. WeΓÇÖve lived. Wollis was wrong ΓÇô this place wasnΓÇÖt a ΓÇ£lesserΓÇ¥ refuge. It was our last refuge.
Why I couldn't help but overhear, this chamber just echoes like mad I have no idea how those stuffy Elders do business in here, but I hear you've met Erza? I would like to present you with an offer.
<sprite name=b> You have my attention.
<sprite name=d> Refuse.
Fantastic, I knew you had a head on your shoulders the moment you walked in here. You see, that ledger of his has information about me I would very much like to... disappear if you catch my meaning.
<sprite name=!s> Sounds like a plan.

Splendid! Trust me, you're doing the right thing. Not just for me, but for everyone who survives this disaster and would rather not have Erza on their backs. But for me especially! You have my gratitude, or will shortly that is.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
You there, may I catch your ear for a moment? Or if you're feeling lucky, would you like to gamble?
<sprite name=b> Yes?
<sprite name=gam> Gamble.
What would you think if this ledger of his say, made its way to me?
Ah, came to me before Erza, you have some good news I'm thinking?
<sprite name=?s> *Hand over Erza's Ledger.*
<sprite name=d> I wasn't able to find it.
Splendid! I'm glad you had the good sense to give the ledger to me rather than him. As a sign of gratitude here is one of the valuables I was able to, ahem, salvage from the city below.
<sprite name=gam> Gamble.
<sprite name=?s> Goodbye.

Welcome back, traveler! I have to say, it feels marvelous to have a clean slate, though I hate to say I don't know how much longer I'll have to enjoy it. In the meantime, would you care to see what else I managed to, ahem, salvage in the chaos?
<sprite name=gam> Gamble.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.

I saw you speaking with Erza again. Were you not swayed by my offer? Oh well. The world is ending so what does it matter? This is all fine. Do you know what would take my mind off the end of the world? Gambling!
<sprite name=gam> Gamble.
<sprite name=d> Goodbye.
I'm telling you, keeping Erza from getting his ledger is the right thing to do. While we wait, care to try your luck?

The new Soul Extractors have proven effective. The yield of essence from the test subjects is reliably greater, and of higher quality compared to previous extraction methods. Yet, it is still far from enough.
The Emperor's Gift is spread thin, every day another of us loses what makes us human and becomes a mindless thrall. Immortal and loyal, but lacking the heart our Emperor defied the gods to defend.
It pains me still that we have resorted to this, but what choice do we have?  Even if the living were willing to accept immortality, would we be able to provide enough essence to sustain them?
We can not let their souls go to waste. If my work can keep the spark of consciousness lit in a single imperial citizen, I will sacrifice as many of the living as I must.

The construction of the Grand Maj'elkan Waystation is nearing completion, and it is all thanks to our slumbering friend. Your decision to excavate this area was simply inspired.
Between the need to keep the titan asleep and the nigh unending well of blood it has provided, the arts of paralytic magic and hemomancy have both been advanced!
Now that we have proven that hemomancy can power our new waypoints, we must expand. I am formally requesting excavation surveys in every corner of the empire. We must find more of these titans!

Once I deciphered the Keepers' movements, the vaults were easy to find. Exploring them proved more challenging. Ancient stonework that could inspire even the greatest of Solarum's architects. Nearly impenetrable.
Between my engineers' finest munitions and the strength of my pet, we managed to crack one openΓÇª Only to find empty halls and vacant chambers.

What would be the purpose of such a stronghold, if it were to house nothing? Yet I could tell they were here, just as I predicted. They are guarding something of immense importance. Something powerful. Rahyeh will be pleased.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Who do the Keepers adhere to?  Our slumbering mother, Eterra? Alone? A goddess they can not see?
They sent no treasure to sea for Lagon, nor works of art south to Majasa. Were they in league with Heorot's savages? No, their alliance seems to be a recent development.
How can they claim a greater connection to Eterra than us? We are the servants of Rahyeh, the inheritor of her domain! It is blasphemy. They will burn.
<sprite name=d> Leave.

I can still feel the flames searing across me. Stealing my wing and my eye. YetΓÇª I saw it. The flames of prophecy revealed that these rats hid a treasure, one far more powerful than anyone could conceive.
Every trinket I've gathered has been a step closer to this moment. The Epoch will be the pinnacle of my collection!
When I present this to Rahyeh, this power, he will finally understand how worthy I am. What I have sacrificed. All for him.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I must remind myself to find suitable provisions for Emberwing. Prey in this area upset a drake's stomachΓÇª bolroth in particular. Could I, I would simply feed him soldiers that question our bond.
I admitΓÇª I have a bias for the creature. He is strong, and more reliable than half those under my command. Plus, we have both been marred by flames.
While my wounds showed me the path to the Epoch, his granted ascendance. Emberwing was raised from his draconic form, reforged in Rahyeh's image.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Heorot's offered protection. More than protection; an alliance. But the truth is, we know little about him or his people. Why should the Council trust him?
Prospect one. He's one of the gods. Eterra made him just as she made us Keepers and gave him a role by her side. He's the guardian of his people, and he does his job well.

Prospect two. He cares for his people. Like the goddess herself, he extends his heart to all who need support. He forms alliances, wanting to stand united. Meanwhile Rahyeh wishes to tear this world asunder.
Prospect three. His people are warriors. Heoborean fighters put ours to shame. We fight because it is necessary, while they relish in it. I'll take on being behind their blade, any day.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.

Quill Hogs. Colorful creatures. Small. They burrow into the ground, and as such, we have yet to encounter many. While they appear close to Keepers, they seem wary of us. Perhaps intelligent creatures? Or anxious ones.
Their quills are more dangerous than one would expect. Up close, they shock with unexpected force. From a distance, they shoot their quills with surprising accuracy. We learned this the hard way.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
Bolroth. Though large and slow, their ability to charge when angry makes them deadly. Avoid as necessary.
They carry an entire ecosystem on their backs. Water deposits above their heads help keep the plants nurtured. Their plants attract through smell, and Bolroth use the plants to trap prey inside.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
We've seen plenty of grole. The Keepers have formed a close bond with these animals. Even when prodded, they pose no threat.
Friendly and easygoing, the grole follow us around. They play games with us. Learn new tricks. Indeed, the Keepers seem to have made a special connection.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
We have successfully taken over their storage rooms. Our engineers are working hard to build golems.
We've dropped in all the materials we need. The Keepers, with their simplistic weaponry, won't stand a chance. Even Heorot's barbarians won't last against our machines.
There were casualties during the transfer. Soldiers flew everything in, but we miscalculated the amount of humans needed. We'll bring their bodies once the operation is done.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Since the Keepers formed, we've held the same rituals. Instructed to keep the Epoch shards separate. Those were Eterra's words.
The first Epoch shard is held in a fortress. Ancient Eterran mechanisms hold it in place, with no one but us knowing how to enter.
The second vault is deep inside the Stonefall mountains. With traps that only the Keepers know how to maneuver.
The third shard is always on the move. It uses multiple vaults; only we possess the knowledge to open them. This is why we can never call a single place "home".
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Let me ask, for how long must we live like this? No one cared about the shards until recently, until Rahyeh. For now, it's just him. But how much longer until Lagon seeks them out? Majasa?
We uphold customs, as Eterra willed. But our goddess is gone. If she knew the state of the world, would she not request us to act? To bring peace back between her children?
We cannot keep the old ways. The council is scaredΓÇª but nothing will change if we do not change.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix are far more militaristic than us. Than our Heoborean allies. The Osprix have ranks, roles, and strategies in how they attack. It makes them deadly.
The closer we are to Osprix camps, the more traps and ambushes we find. I'm writing this in case I don't make it outΓÇª to whoever finds us, I'm sorry. We tried our best.
The Osprix can mimic our call. They build devices that far exceed our own. Despite the weight of their equipment, they carry it with ease. Our cliffs mean nothing to those who can fly.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix value luxury. It's as evident in Solarum as it is here. Our tents bear the symbolic reds and golds. Machines, forged from the finest steel. They contrast the simplicity of Keeper ruins.

I am lucky to be a servant to Rahyeh. I am only human, yet he takes me under his wing and gives me knowledge. The means to advance my craft. His grace rewards me far more than other humans can imagine.
It feels evident. Though Rahyeh adored her, Eterra's gone. This world needs a new leader. Where we join under one rule; Rahyeh's rule. I anticipate his reign.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I once believed the shards would be safe where the gods could not reach. In the future. I was mistaken. I find myself in a ruined era.
With only one shard left by my side, my purpose is clear. I must seek refuge. Prevent others from this power, and locate the rest of the Epoch.

I offered them shelter. The tunnels and Keeper ruins are still navigational, and with their helpΓÇª we can build something new.
Here, I will guard what little of the Epoch I have left, and find a way to protect us. This is not the end.
<sprite name=d> Leave.

It is time I hang up the title of Elder Balthas. With that, I begin again.
Here, I leave my knowledge. The records of Eterra's past, and the millennia of wrong doings that created this world.

The Emperor's Gift is spread thin, every day another of us loses what makes us human and becomes a mindless thrall. Immortal and loyal, but lacking the heart our Emperor defied the gods to defend.
It pains me still that we have resorted to this, but what choice do we have?  Even if the living were willing to accept immortality, would we be able to provide enough essence to sustain them?

Between my engineers' finest munitions and the strength of my pet, we managed to crack one openΓÇª Only to find empty halls and vacant chambers.

What would be the purpose of such a stronghold, if it were to house nothing? Yet I could tell they were here, just as I predicted. They are guarding something of immense importance. Something powerful. Rahyeh will be pleased.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Who do the Keepers adhere to?  Our slumbering mother, Eterra? Alone? A goddess they can not see?
They sent no treasure to sea for Lagon, nor works of art south to Majasa. Were they in league with Heorot's savages? No, their alliance seems to be a recent development.
How can they claim a greater connection to Eterra than us? We are the servants of Rahyeh, the inheritor of her domain! It is blasphemy. They will burn.
<sprite name=d> Leave.

I can still feel the flames searing across me. Stealing my wing and my eye. YetΓÇª I saw it. The flames of prophecy revealed that these rats hid a treasure, one far more powerful than anyone could conceive.
Every trinket I've gathered has been a step closer to this moment. The Epoch will be the pinnacle of my collection!
When I present this to Rahyeh, this power, he will finally understand how worthy I am. What I have sacrificed. All for him.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I must remind myself to find suitable provisions for Emberwing. Prey in this area upset a drake's stomachΓÇª bolroth in particular. Could I, I would simply feed him soldiers that question our bond.
I admitΓÇª I have a bias for the creature. He is strong, and more reliable than half those under my command. Plus, we have both been marred by flames.
While my wounds showed me the path to the Epoch, his granted ascendance. Emberwing was raised from his draconic form, reforged in Rahyeh's image.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Heorot's offered protection. More than protection; an alliance. But the truth is, we know little about him or his people. Why should the Council trust him?
Prospect one. He's one of the gods. Eterra made him just as she made us Keepers and gave him a role by her side. He's the guardian of his people, and he does his job well.

Prospect two. He cares for his people. Like the goddess herself, he extends his heart to all who need support. He forms alliances, wanting to stand united. Meanwhile Rahyeh wishes to tear this world asunder.
Prospect three. His people are warriors. Heoborean fighters put ours to shame. We fight because it is necessary, while they relish in it. I'll take on being behind their blade, any day.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.

Quill Hogs. Colorful creatures. Small. They burrow into the ground, and as such, we have yet to encounter many. While they appear close to Keepers, they seem wary of us. Perhaps intelligent creatures? Or anxious ones.
Their quills are more dangerous than one would expect. Up close, they shock with unexpected force. From a distance, they shoot their quills with surprising accuracy. We learned this the hard way.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
Bolroth. Though large and slow, their ability to charge when angry makes them deadly. Avoid as necessary.
They carry an entire ecosystem on their backs. Water deposits above their heads help keep the plants nurtured. Their plants attract through smell, and Bolroth use the plants to trap prey inside.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
We've seen plenty of grole. The Keepers have formed a close bond with these animals. Even when prodded, they pose no threat.
Friendly and easygoing, the grole follow us around. They play games with us. Learn new tricks. Indeed, the Keepers seem to have made a special connection.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
We have successfully taken over their storage rooms. Our engineers are working hard to build golems.
We've dropped in all the materials we need. The Keepers, with their simplistic weaponry, won't stand a chance. Even Heorot's barbarians won't last against our machines.
There were casualties during the transfer. Soldiers flew everything in, but we miscalculated the amount of humans needed. We'll bring their bodies once the operation is done.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Since the Keepers formed, we've held the same rituals. Instructed to keep the Epoch shards separate. Those were Eterra's words.
The first Epoch shard is held in a fortress. Ancient Eterran mechanisms hold it in place, with no one but us knowing how to enter.
The second vault is deep inside the Stonefall mountains. With traps that only the Keepers know how to maneuver.
The third shard is always on the move. It uses multiple vaults; only we possess the knowledge to open them. This is why we can never call a single place "home".
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Let me ask, for how long must we live like this? No one cared about the shards until recently, until Rahyeh. For now, it's just him. But how much longer until Lagon seeks them out? Majasa?
We uphold customs, as Eterra willed. But our goddess is gone. If she knew the state of the world, would she not request us to act? To bring peace back between her children?
We cannot keep the old ways. The council is scaredΓÇª but nothing will change if we do not change.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix are far more militaristic than us. Than our Heoborean allies. The Osprix have ranks, roles, and strategies in how they attack. It makes them deadly.

<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix value luxury. It's as evident in Solarum as it is here. Our tents bear the symbolic reds and golds. Machines, forged from the finest steel. They contrast the simplicity of Keeper ruins.

I am lucky to be a servant to Rahyeh. I am only human, yet he takes me under his wing and gives me knowledge. The means to advance my craft. His grace rewards me far more than other humans can imagine.
It feels evident. Though Rahyeh adored her, Eterra's gone. This world needs a new leader. Where we join under one rule; Rahyeh's rule. I anticipate his reign.

I once believed the shards would be safe where the gods could not reach. In the future. I was mistaken. I find myself in a ruined era.

Once I deciphered the Keepers' movements, the vaults were easy to find. Exploring them proved more challenging. Ancient stonework that could inspire even the greatest of Solarum's architects. Nearly impenetrable.
Between my engineers' finest munitions and the strength of my pet, we managed to crack one openΓÇª Only to find empty halls and vacant chambers.

What would be the purpose of such a stronghold, if it were to house nothing? Yet I could tell they were here, just as I predicted. They are guarding something of immense importance. Something powerful. Rahyeh will be pleased.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Who do the Keepers adhere to?  Our slumbering mother, Eterra? Alone? A goddess they can not see?
They sent no treasure to sea for Lagon, nor works of art south to Majasa. Were they in league with Heorot's savages? No, their alliance seems to be a recent development.
How can they claim a greater connection to Eterra than us? We are the servants of Rahyeh, the inheritor of her domain! It is blasphemy. They will burn.
<sprite name=d> Leave.

I can still feel the flames searing across me. Stealing my wing and my eye. YetΓÇª I saw it. The flames of prophecy revealed that these rats hid a treasure, one far more powerful than anyone could conceive.
Every trinket I've gathered has been a step closer to this moment. The Epoch will be the pinnacle of my collection!
When I present this to Rahyeh, this power, he will finally understand how worthy I am. What I have sacrificed. All for him.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I must remind myself to find suitable provisions for Emberwing. Prey in this area upset a drake's stomachΓÇª bolroth in particular. Could I, I would simply feed him soldiers that question our bond.
I admitΓÇª I have a bias for the creature. He is strong, and more reliable than half those under my command. Plus, we have both been marred by flames.
While my wounds showed me the path to the Epoch, his granted ascendance. Emberwing was raised from his draconic form, reforged in Rahyeh's image.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Heorot's offered protection. More than protection; an alliance. But the truth is, we know little about him or his people. Why should the Council trust him?
Prospect one. He's one of the gods. Eterra made him just as she made us Keepers and gave him a role by her side. He's the guardian of his people, and he does his job well.

Prospect two. He cares for his people. Like the goddess herself, he extends his heart to all who need support. He forms alliances, wanting to stand united. Meanwhile Rahyeh wishes to tear this world asunder.
Prospect three. His people are warriors. Heoborean fighters put ours to shame. We fight because it is necessary, while they relish in it. I'll take on being behind their blade, any day.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.

Quill Hogs. Colorful creatures. Small. They burrow into the ground, and as such, we have yet to encounter many. While they appear close to Keepers, they seem wary of us. Perhaps intelligent creatures? Or anxious ones.
Their quills are more dangerous than one would expect. Up close, they shock with unexpected force. From a distance, they shoot their quills with surprising accuracy. We learned this the hard way.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
Bolroth. Though large and slow, their ability to charge when angry makes them deadly. Avoid as necessary.
They carry an entire ecosystem on their backs. Water deposits above their heads help keep the plants nurtured. Their plants attract through smell, and Bolroth use the plants to trap prey inside.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
We've seen plenty of grole. The Keepers have formed a close bond with these animals. Even when prodded, they pose no threat.
Friendly and easygoing, the grole follow us around. They play games with us. Learn new tricks. Indeed, the Keepers seem to have made a special connection.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
We have successfully taken over their storage rooms. Our engineers are working hard to build golems.
We've dropped in all the materials we need. The Keepers, with their simplistic weaponry, won't stand a chance. Even Heorot's barbarians won't last against our machines.
There were casualties during the transfer. Soldiers flew everything in, but we miscalculated the amount of humans needed. We'll bring their bodies once the operation is done.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Since the Keepers formed, we've held the same rituals. Instructed to keep the Epoch shards separate. Those were Eterra's words.
The first Epoch shard is held in a fortress. Ancient Eterran mechanisms hold it in place, with no one but us knowing how to enter.
The second vault is deep inside the Stonefall mountains. With traps that only the Keepers know how to maneuver.
The third shard is always on the move. It uses multiple vaults; only we possess the knowledge to open them. This is why we can never call a single place "home".
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Let me ask, for how long must we live like this? No one cared about the shards until recently, until Rahyeh. For now, it's just him. But how much longer until Lagon seeks them out? Majasa?
We uphold customs, as Eterra willed. But our goddess is gone. If she knew the state of the world, would she not request us to act? To bring peace back between her children?
We cannot keep the old ways. The council is scaredΓÇª but nothing will change if we do not change.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix are far more militaristic than us. Than our Heoborean allies. The Osprix have ranks, roles, and strategies in how they attack. It makes them deadly.
The closer we are to Osprix camps, the more traps and ambushes we find. I'm writing this in case I don't make it outΓÇª to whoever finds us, I'm sorry. We tried our best.
The Osprix can mimic our call. They build devices that far exceed our own. Despite the weight of their equipment, they carry it with ease. Our cliffs mean nothing to those who can fly.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix value luxury. It's as evident in Solarum as it is here. Our tents bear the symbolic reds and golds. Machines, forged from the finest steel. They contrast the simplicity of Keeper ruins.

I am lucky to be a servant to Rahyeh. I am only human, yet he takes me under his wing and gives me knowledge. The means to advance my craft. His grace rewards me far more than other humans can imagine.
It feels evident. Though Rahyeh adored her, Eterra's gone. This world needs a new leader. Where we join under one rule; Rahyeh's rule. I anticipate his reign.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I once believed the shards would be safe where the gods could not reach. In the future. I was mistaken. I find myself in a ruined era.
With only one shard left by my side, my purpose is clear. I must seek refuge. Prevent others from this power, and locate the rest of the Epoch.

Here, I will guard what little of the Epoch I have left, and find a way to protect us. This is not the end.

The Emperor's Gift is spread thin, every day another of us loses what makes us human and becomes a mindless thrall. Immortal and loyal, but lacking the heart our Emperor defied the gods to defend.
It pains me still that we have resorted to this, but what choice do we have?  Even if the living were willing to accept immortality, would we be able to provide enough essence to sustain them?
We can not let their souls go to waste. If my work can keep the spark of consciousness lit in a single imperial citizen, I will sacrifice as many of the living as I must.

Once I deciphered the Keepers' movements, the vaults were easy to find. Exploring them proved more challenging. Ancient stonework that could inspire even the greatest of Solarum's architects. Nearly impenetrable.
Between my engineers' finest munitions and the strength of my pet, we managed to crack one openΓÇª Only to find empty halls and vacant chambers.

What would be the purpose of such a stronghold, if it were to house nothing? Yet I could tell they were here, just as I predicted. They are guarding something of immense importance. Something powerful. Rahyeh will be pleased.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Who do the Keepers adhere to?  Our slumbering mother, Eterra? Alone? A goddess they can not see?
They sent no treasure to sea for Lagon, nor works of art south to Majasa. Were they in league with Heorot's savages? No, their alliance seems to be a recent development.
How can they claim a greater connection to Eterra than us? We are the servants of Rahyeh, the inheritor of her domain! It is blasphemy. They will burn.
<sprite name=d> Leave.

I can still feel the flames searing across me. Stealing my wing and my eye. YetΓÇª I saw it. The flames of prophecy revealed that these rats hid a treasure, one far more powerful than anyone could conceive.
Every trinket I've gathered has been a step closer to this moment. The Epoch will be the pinnacle of my collection!
When I present this to Rahyeh, this power, he will finally understand how worthy I am. What I have sacrificed. All for him.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I must remind myself to find suitable provisions for Emberwing. Prey in this area upset a drake's stomachΓÇª bolroth in particular. Could I, I would simply feed him soldiers that question our bond.
I admitΓÇª I have a bias for the creature. He is strong, and more reliable than half those under my command. Plus, we have both been marred by flames.
While my wounds showed me the path to the Epoch, his granted ascendance. Emberwing was raised from his draconic form, reforged in Rahyeh's image.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Heorot's offered protection. More than protection; an alliance. But the truth is, we know little about him or his people. Why should the Council trust him?
Prospect one. He's one of the gods. Eterra made him just as she made us Keepers and gave him a role by her side. He's the guardian of his people, and he does his job well.

Prospect two. He cares for his people. Like the goddess herself, he extends his heart to all who need support. He forms alliances, wanting to stand united. Meanwhile Rahyeh wishes to tear this world asunder.
Prospect three. His people are warriors. Heoborean fighters put ours to shame. We fight because it is necessary, while they relish in it. I'll take on being behind their blade, any day.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.

Quill Hogs. Colorful creatures. Small. They burrow into the ground, and as such, we have yet to encounter many. While they appear close to Keepers, they seem wary of us. Perhaps intelligent creatures? Or anxious ones.
Their quills are more dangerous than one would expect. Up close, they shock with unexpected force. From a distance, they shoot their quills with surprising accuracy. We learned this the hard way.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
Bolroth. Though large and slow, their ability to charge when angry makes them deadly. Avoid as necessary.
They carry an entire ecosystem on their backs. Water deposits above their heads help keep the plants nurtured. Their plants attract through smell, and Bolroth use the plants to trap prey inside.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
We've seen plenty of grole. The Keepers have formed a close bond with these animals. Even when prodded, they pose no threat.
Friendly and easygoing, the grole follow us around. They play games with us. Learn new tricks. Indeed, the Keepers seem to have made a special connection.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
We have successfully taken over their storage rooms. Our engineers are working hard to build golems.
We've dropped in all the materials we need. The Keepers, with their simplistic weaponry, won't stand a chance. Even Heorot's barbarians won't last against our machines.
There were casualties during the transfer. Soldiers flew everything in, but we miscalculated the amount of humans needed. We'll bring their bodies once the operation is done.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Since the Keepers formed, we've held the same rituals. Instructed to keep the Epoch shards separate. Those were Eterra's words.
The first Epoch shard is held in a fortress. Ancient Eterran mechanisms hold it in place, with no one but us knowing how to enter.
The second vault is deep inside the Stonefall mountains. With traps that only the Keepers know how to maneuver.
The third shard is always on the move. It uses multiple vaults; only we possess the knowledge to open them. This is why we can never call a single place "home".
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Let me ask, for how long must we live like this? No one cared about the shards until recently, until Rahyeh. For now, it's just him. But how much longer until Lagon seeks them out? Majasa?
We uphold customs, as Eterra willed. But our goddess is gone. If she knew the state of the world, would she not request us to act? To bring peace back between her children?
We cannot keep the old ways. The council is scaredΓÇª but nothing will change if we do not change.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix are far more militaristic than us. Than our Heoborean allies. The Osprix have ranks, roles, and strategies in how they attack. It makes them deadly.
The closer we are to Osprix camps, the more traps and ambushes we find. I'm writing this in case I don't make it outΓÇª to whoever finds us, I'm sorry. We tried our best.
The Osprix can mimic our call. They build devices that far exceed our own. Despite the weight of their equipment, they carry it with ease. Our cliffs mean nothing to those who can fly.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix value luxury. It's as evident in Solarum as it is here. Our tents bear the symbolic reds and golds. Machines, forged from the finest steel. They contrast the simplicity of Keeper ruins.

I am lucky to be a servant to Rahyeh. I am only human, yet he takes me under his wing and gives me knowledge. The means to advance my craft. His grace rewards me far more than other humans can imagine.
It feels evident. Though Rahyeh adored her, Eterra's gone. This world needs a new leader. Where we join under one rule; Rahyeh's rule. I anticipate his reign.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I once believed the shards would be safe where the gods could not reach. In the future. I was mistaken. I find myself in a ruined era.
With only one shard left by my side, my purpose is clear. I must seek refuge. Prevent others from this power, and locate the rest of the Epoch.
What happened when I tapped into its power? Why did one shard come with me? Where are the others? Was all of this for nothing?

It turns out, I am not entirely alone. There are people who seek refuge; a means for survival. Given these were once the Ulatri mountainsΓÇª Is this what becomes of my people?
I offered them shelter. The tunnels and Keeper ruins are still navigational, and with their helpΓÇª we can build something new.
Here, I will guard what little of the Epoch I have left, and find a way to protect us. This is not the end.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
As quickly as I came here, it is now time to go. Last Refuge is as safe as it can be. We've created a sanctuary here.
It is time I hang up the title of Elder Balthas. With that, I begin again.
Here, I leave my knowledge. The records of Eterra's past, and the millennia of wrong doings that created this world.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
If you read this, I am gone. Keep Last Refuge safe. Fight off the void. Do not succumb to despair.
I trust you all, you are my people. Which is why I must go. May Eterra protect you all.

Between my engineers' finest munitions and the strength of my pet, we managed to crack one openΓÇª Only to find empty halls and vacant chambers.

What would be the purpose of such a stronghold, if it were to house nothing? Yet I could tell they were here, just as I predicted. They are guarding something of immense importance. Something powerful. Rahyeh will be pleased.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Who do the Keepers adhere to?  Our slumbering mother, Eterra? Alone? A goddess they can not see?
They sent no treasure to sea for Lagon, nor works of art south to Majasa. Were they in league with Heorot's savages? No, their alliance seems to be a recent development.
How can they claim a greater connection to Eterra than us? We are the servants of Rahyeh, the inheritor of her domain! It is blasphemy. They will burn.
<sprite name=d> Leave.

I can still feel the flames searing across me. Stealing my wing and my eye. YetΓÇª I saw it. The flames of prophecy revealed that these rats hid a treasure, one far more powerful than anyone could conceive.
Every trinket I've gathered has been a step closer to this moment. The Epoch will be the pinnacle of my collection!
When I present this to Rahyeh, this power, he will finally understand how worthy I am. What I have sacrificed. All for him.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I must remind myself to find suitable provisions for Emberwing. Prey in this area upset a drake's stomachΓÇª bolroth in particular. Could I, I would simply feed him soldiers that question our bond.
I admitΓÇª I have a bias for the creature. He is strong, and more reliable than half those under my command. Plus, we have both been marred by flames.
While my wounds showed me the path to the Epoch, his granted ascendance. Emberwing was raised from his draconic form, reforged in Rahyeh's image.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Heorot's offered protection. More than protection; an alliance. But the truth is, we know little about him or his people. Why should the Council trust him?
Prospect one. He's one of the gods. Eterra made him just as she made us Keepers and gave him a role by her side. He's the guardian of his people, and he does his job well.

Prospect two. He cares for his people. Like the goddess herself, he extends his heart to all who need support. He forms alliances, wanting to stand united. Meanwhile Rahyeh wishes to tear this world asunder.
Prospect three. His people are warriors. Heoborean fighters put ours to shame. We fight because it is necessary, while they relish in it. I'll take on being behind their blade, any day.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.

Quill Hogs. Colorful creatures. Small. They burrow into the ground, and as such, we have yet to encounter many. While they appear close to Keepers, they seem wary of us. Perhaps intelligent creatures? Or anxious ones.
Their quills are more dangerous than one would expect. Up close, they shock with unexpected force. From a distance, they shoot their quills with surprising accuracy. We learned this the hard way.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
Bolroth. Though large and slow, their ability to charge when angry makes them deadly. Avoid as necessary.
They carry an entire ecosystem on their backs. Water deposits above their heads help keep the plants nurtured. Their plants attract through smell, and Bolroth use the plants to trap prey inside.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Most of the animals in this region are harmless. Still, several will pack a punch if you cross them. To prepare warriors coming south, here are some warnings.
We've seen plenty of grole. The Keepers have formed a close bond with these animals. Even when prodded, they pose no threat.
Friendly and easygoing, the grole follow us around. They play games with us. Learn new tricks. Indeed, the Keepers seem to have made a special connection.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
We have successfully taken over their storage rooms. Our engineers are working hard to build golems.
We've dropped in all the materials we need. The Keepers, with their simplistic weaponry, won't stand a chance. Even Heorot's barbarians won't last against our machines.
There were casualties during the transfer. Soldiers flew everything in, but we miscalculated the amount of humans needed. We'll bring their bodies once the operation is done.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Since the Keepers formed, we've held the same rituals. Instructed to keep the Epoch shards separate. Those were Eterra's words.
The first Epoch shard is held in a fortress. Ancient Eterran mechanisms hold it in place, with no one but us knowing how to enter.
The second vault is deep inside the Stonefall mountains. With traps that only the Keepers know how to maneuver.
The third shard is always on the move. It uses multiple vaults; only we possess the knowledge to open them. This is why we can never call a single place "home".
<sprite name=d> Leave.
Let me ask, for how long must we live like this? No one cared about the shards until recently, until Rahyeh. For now, it's just him. But how much longer until Lagon seeks them out? Majasa?
We uphold customs, as Eterra willed. But our goddess is gone. If she knew the state of the world, would she not request us to act? To bring peace back between her children?
We cannot keep the old ways. The council is scaredΓÇª but nothing will change if we do not change.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix are far more militaristic than us. Than our Heoborean allies. The Osprix have ranks, roles, and strategies in how they attack. It makes them deadly.

The Osprix can mimic our call. They build devices that far exceed our own. Despite the weight of their equipment, they carry it with ease. Our cliffs mean nothing to those who can fly.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
The Osprix value luxury. It's as evident in Solarum as it is here. Our tents bear the symbolic reds and golds. Machines, forged from the finest steel. They contrast the simplicity of Keeper ruins.

I am lucky to be a servant to Rahyeh. I am only human, yet he takes me under his wing and gives me knowledge. The means to advance my craft. His grace rewards me far more than other humans can imagine.
It feels evident. Though Rahyeh adored her, Eterra's gone. This world needs a new leader. Where we join under one rule; Rahyeh's rule. I anticipate his reign.
<sprite name=d> Leave.
I once believed the shards would be safe where the gods could not reach. In the future. I was mistaken. I find myself in a ruined era.
With only one shard left by my side, my purpose is clear. I must seek refuge. Prevent others from this power, and locate the rest of the Epoch.

Here, I will guard what little of the Epoch I have left, and find a way to protect us. This is not the end.

Here, I leave my knowledge. The records of Eterra's past, and the millennia of wrong doings that created this world.
